description: Initial multilingual ground-truth set for testing VAD + ASR quality across English, Hindi, and Hinglish.
metricsNote: WER, CER, and word accuracy must only be calculated when a ground truth item is selected. Hindi and Hinglish may require normalized WER later because Devanagari and Romanized transcripts can represent similar meaning with different scripts.

items.expectedTranscript: Hello everyone. Today we are testing speech recognition.
items.notes: Use this as the simplest first sanity check for the ASR pipeline.

items.expectedTranscript: My order number is A one seven five nine. Please check the delivery status.
items.testPurpose: English alphanumeric and order ID capture.
items.notes: Useful for testing whether ASR preserves letter and number sequences clearly.

items.expectedTranscript: I will pay tomorrow, but please send the payment link on WhatsApp.
items.testPurpose: English business intent with payment and WhatsApp entities.
items.notes: Represents a common commerce or collections call utterance.

items.expectedTranscript: नमस्ते, मुझे अपने ऑर्डर की डिलीवरी स्थिति जाननी है।
items.testPurpose: Clean Hindi baseline with delivery intent.
items.notes: Canonical truth is Devanagari. Raw WER should compare against this exact script.

items.expectedTranscript: कृपया मुझे पेमेंट लिंक व्हाट्सएप पर भेज दीजिए।
items.testPurpose: Hindi sentence with common English business terms.
items.notes: Tests whether ASR handles Hindi speech containing business terms like payment link and WhatsApp.

items.expectedTranscript: मेरा मोबाइल नंबर नौ आठ सात छह पांच चार तीन दो एक शून्य है।
items.notes: Important for testing phone numbers, IDs, policy numbers, and other digit-heavy call flows.

items.expectedTranscript: मेरा order कल deliver हो जाएगा क्या?
items.notes: Canonical Hinglish truth is Roman script.

items.expectedTranscript: हाँ, payment link WhatsApp पे भेज दो, मैं शाम तक pay कर दूँगा.
items.testPurpose: Hinglish payment intent with WhatsApp entity.
items.notes: Tests code-mixed business speech that is common in Indian customer calls.

items.expectedTranscript: Ek second, मुझे समझ नहीं आया, number के आगे क्या लिखा है?
items.testPurpose: Hinglish hesitation and unclear field reference.
items.notes: Useful for testing messy real-call utterances where the user is confused or reading something unclear.

items.expectedTranscript: हाँ हाँ, मैं सुन रहा हूँ, आप continue करो.
items.notes: Important for later VAD/turn-taking tests. This should not automatically be treated as a full user intent.
